page: Microsoft Azure E8ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the EPSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. The memory configuration provides 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate balanced performance across several workloads, with average ratings in stress-ng single-core (1822.39) and multi-core (14507.4) tests. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 565.27 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, and 74.12 GB/sec for large-block reads. However, certain computational tasks show limitations; the instance scores in the weak category for Geekbench single-core (1056.0) and Passmark CPU (6118.83) tests. Bzip3 decompression also registers as poor (7.32 GB/sec single-threaded) to weak (34.54 GB/sec multi-threaded).

The E8ps_v5 offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require x86-specific instruction sets, such as AVX or AMX, which are absent on this ARM64 architecture. It demonstrates average performance in database operations (Passmark score of 5372.49) and Redis SET operations (11.24 million operations per second), making it suitable for caching layers, relational databases, and static web serving (10.41 GB/sec throughput). Conversely, it is less suited for large language model inference, where text generation speeds for even small models fall into the poor to weak categories.
description: The Microsoft Azure E8ps_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the EPSv5 family, utilizing a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a memory-optimized 8.0 GB per core ratio, but lacks bundled local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmarks show average performance in multi-core processing, static web serving, and database operations, alongside high cached memory read bandwidth of 565.27 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in single-core execution, cryptographic tasks, and LLM text generation. Lacking x86 vector extensions, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, ARM-native workloads such as database management, caching layers, and web serving, while being less suited for intensive mathematical computations or machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores and 64 GB of RAM. It delivers average database and web serving performance with no local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 8 Ampere cores, 64 GB RAM, and average database performance, but no local storage.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for database operations, caching, and web serving workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores running at 3.0 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
No bundled local storage, but supports Premium Storage integration
Average performance in database operations and static web serving
Weak single-core and cryptographic benchmark results
Suited for caching layers, relational databases, and ARM-native applications
